lines: You are working on TraceCrumb, a Next.js App Router project with Supabase.
- Incidents, events, patterns tables exist and are live
- Divergence is computed at read time from step_order = 1 event
- There is NO artifacts table and NO artifact generation yet
- The resolve flow does NOT capture a root cause note
YOUR TASK: Close the learning loop by adding root cause capture and artifact generation.
STEP 1 — Run this in Supabase SQL editor:
ALTER TABLE incidents ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS root_cause_note TEXT;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS artifacts (
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
incident_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES incidents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_artifacts_incident_id
- Accept optional root_cause_note in request body
- Write root_cause_note to the incidents table on resolve
- After writing the incident update, call generateAndPersistArtifact(incidentId)
- Return the updated incident as before
export async function generateAndPersistArtifact(incidentId: string): Promise<void>
2. Fetch all events for the incident ordered by step_order ASC
- diverged = suggested !== null && actual !== null && suggested !== actual
Each element: { step: number, message: string, timestamp: string }
7. Upsert into artifacts table (use incident_id as conflict key)
- Query artifacts table WHERE incident_id = id
- Return 200 with artifact row if found
- Return 404 if not found (incident not yet resolved or artifact not generated)
STEP 5 — Update the resolve UI on the incident page
Current resolve section: two buttons (success / failure) with confirm step.
1. Two buttons remain: "Resolve as success" / "Resolve as failure"
2. Clicking either triggers the confirm step AS BEFORE, but now also reveals:
- A textarea: "What was the actual root cause?" (optional, placeholder text)
- The confirm button now reads "Resolve & Generate Report"
4. After successful resolve: do NOT just reload. Instead redirect to
This is the artifact view page. Server component.
- Service name + outcome badge (SUCCESS in green / FAILURE in red)
- TTR displayed prominently: "Resolved in X minutes"
- If diverged: show a two-column comparison card:
- If not diverged: "First action matched suggestion." (muted)
- Render event_sequence from the JSONB field
- Small muted note: "This incident's first action has been recorded
- No scores, no charts — just confirmation the loop ran
- Match existing TraceCrumb dark theme exactly
- Monospace for service names, step numbers, code-like content
- No new color tokens — use what already exists
- This page should feel like a clean terminal printout, not a dashboard
- generateAndPersistArtifact must be called ONLY from the resolve API route — not from the client
- Artifact generation is synchronous within the resolve request — do not defer it to a background job
- The artifacts table has a UNIQUE index on incident_id — use upsert, not insert
- markdown_export must be a complete, copy-pasteable string with no JSX — plain text with markdown syntax only
- Do not add any new navigation items to the main nav
- Do not touch the patterns table or reinforcement logic in this prompt
You are working on TraceCrumb. The artifact loop is now closed.
- Suggestions are displayed as a list on the incident page
- The action log textarea requires manual typing
- suggested_action is captured from the first event's pre-fill state
YOUR TASK: Make suggestions clickable so they pre-fill the action textarea.
Each suggestion item gets an onClick handler.
Clicking it: sets the action textarea value to the suggestion text.
Visual feedback on click: brief highlight on the suggestion item (border flash or
background pulse — one CSS transition, not a library).
The textarea should scroll into view after pre-fill (element.scrollIntoView).
Add a small hint below the suggestion list (only on the first event,
i.e. when no events exist yet):
"↑ Click a suggestion to pre-fill your first action"
Remove this hint once the first event has been logged.
Do not change the POST logic.
Do not change how suggested_action is captured — it already reads from
the textarea value at submit time, so pre-filling the textarea is sufficient.
Do not change the suggestion fetch logic.
- This is a UI-only change — no API modifications
- The pre-fill must work for both heuristic and historical suggestions
- Do not auto-submit — clicking a suggestion only pre-fills, user still clicks "Log action"
